articles.title: JavaScript Engines Zoo: Learn About Over 100 JS Engines
articles.description: I’m a sucker for a big table of data and this is about as big as it gets when it comes to JavaScript engines. See how various engines compare, sort them by performance, or click on an engine’s name to learn more about its development, history, and end users. The project’s repo also has Dockerfiles for trying each of them out.
articles.author: Ivan Krasilnikov

articles.title: Valdi: Snap's Newly-Open Cross-Platform UI Framework
articles.description: The team behind Snapchat has open sourced this cross-platform UI framework that it’s used in its production apps for eight years: “Write your UI once in declarative TypeScript, and it compiles directly to native views on iOS, Android, and macOS—no web views, no JavaScript bridges.”
articles.author: Snap

articles.title: ▶  The State of Node.js in 2025, Explained
articles.description: A thirty-minute talk from JSNation earlier this year where TSC member Matteo Collina presented an update on Node’s still-growing popularity, release schedule, security, recent performance enhancements, the permissions system, and more.
articles.author: GitNation

articles.title: V8's Garbage Collector Developments in Recent Years
articles.description: Andy, who’s worked on both V8 and JavaScriptCore in the past, reviews the major developments in the V8 engine’s garbage collector over the past couple of years. Very technical, but a valuable piece of history.
articles.author: Andy Wingo

articles.title: Comparing Electron vs. Tauri for Building and Distributing a Desktop App
articles.description: A team working on an open-source desktop app built using JavaScript and Electron took a look into the Rust-based Tauri instead, with mixed, but mostly positive, results.
articles.author: Eric Richardson

articles.title: Using Expo for React Native Development in 2025: A Perspective
articles.description: Expo has essentially become to React Native what Next.js became to React. But is it well suited for you? Jack weighs the pros and cons.
articles.author: Jack Rosa

articles.title: imgui-react-runtime: React + Dear ImGui + Static Hermes
articles.description: When the author teased a demo of this on X a few weeks ago, I wasn’t sure if it would get released, but here it is. A new way to put together native apps using React and the popular lightweight GUI library Dear ImGui.
articles.author: Tzvetan Mikov

articles.title: ESLint Plugin for Baseline JavaScript
articles.description: The winner of last month's Baseline Tooling Hackathon is an ESLint plugin that flags code that’s using features newer than your configured Baseline target (features broadly supported by modern browsers).
articles.author: Ryuya Hasegawa

articles.title: pnpm 10.21: Safer Installs and Smarter Runtime Management
articles.description: Now installs the Node version required by a dependency, declared in its engines.runtime field, meaning CLI apps and postinstall scripts will run with the specified version. The trustPolicy setting also adds protection against supply-chain attacks by failing to install a package if its trust level drops.
articles.author: Zoltan Kochan

articles.title: Ink 6.5: Build Interactive CLI Apps using React
articles.description: A very widely used terminal-based React renderer for building reactive terminal apps using components. v6.5 is notable for adding an incremental rendering option.
articles.author: Vadim Demedes

articles.description: The TC39 committee behind ECMAScript is meeting for the 111th time next week and has a packed agenda to discuss. (Thanks to Rob Palmer for the heads up.)
For Stack Overflow, Tanya Janca shares several tips for writing more secure JavaScript.
The co-founder of the fantastic Val Town online JavaScript platform shares a retrospective of its past three years, as well as two roles they're hiring for.
Visual Types is a handy visually-oriented guide/tour of what types are and how they work, with a focus on TypeScript.

Bun v1.3.2 – Isolated package installs, introduced as the default in v1.3, were causing issues with some existing projects, so now hoisted installs are back as the default for those. bun install also gets even faster and you can do CPU profiling by running with --cpu-prof.
Node.js v25.2.0 (Current) – Now with type stripping marked as stable.
esbuild 0.27 – Popular bundler. A notable release for having breaking changes (so you're reminded to pin esbuild appropriately).
TanStack DB 0.5, Node.js v24.11.1 (LTS), MikroORM 6.6

articles.title: The Web Animation Performance Tier List
articles.description: A comprehensive and detailed guide in which Matt shares everything he knows about animation performance, covering the render pipeline, hardware acceleration, and more. Animations are ranked in a tier list, noting how various techniques impact things. A must read if you’re striving for a smooth, responsive UI.
articles.author: Matt Perry

articles.title: High-Performance Syntax Highlighting with the CSS Custom Highlight API
articles.description: Now supported in all major browsers, the CSS Custom Highlight API provides a mechanism for styling arbitrary text ranges in a document by using JavaScript to create the ranges, and CSS to style them.
articles.author: Pavitra Golchha

articles.title: The Weird Parts of position: sticky;
articles.description: A highly useful, but often tricky CSS feature to get 100% right. Adam runs through how it works, and highlights how to tackle some common points of frustration.
articles.author: Adam Rackis

articles.title: pathLength Makes SVG Path Animations Easier to Manage
articles.description: A quick tip on how to combine stroke-dasharray, stroke-dashoffset and CSS transitions to effectively animate an SVG path.
articles.author: Stefan Judis

articles.title: Perfectly Pointed Tooltips: To the Corners
articles.description: We shared the first two parts of this series on tooltips last week, and here’s the latest, this time focusing on perfecting the corners.
articles.author: Temani Afif

articles.title: Programming Principles for Self Taught Frontend Developers
articles.description: Some solid, grounded, actionable rule-of-thumb suggestions that should straighten up or improve your workflow.
articles.author: Kilian Valkhof

articles.title: Effectively Monitoring Web Performance
articles.description: Outlines a strategy for web perf optimization and explains the roles that different types of data play in it.
articles.author: Matt Zeunert

articles.title: “A.I.” Browsers: The Price of Admission Is Too High
articles.description: “If data is the fuel for A.I., what is the product?”
articles.author: Bruce Lawson Opinion

articles.title: Umami 3.0: Self-Hosted, Privacy-Focused Google Analytics Alternative
articles.description: A self-hosting alternative to the likes of Plausible and Google Analytics. Here’s a full feature list. It's also available as a paid hosted service.
articles.author: Umami Software, Inc.

articles.title: Travels: A Fast, Framework-Agnostic Undo/Redo Library
articles.description: Allows you to add undo/redo functionality to apps like text editors, drawing tools, or other interactive software. Boasts better memory management by only storing changes, rather than full snapshots.
articles.author: mutative

articles.title: FileMock: A Free Test File Generator to Add Test Data to Your Apps
articles.description: Allows you to generate videos, audio clips, images, and documents in dozens of formats and customizable to fit your testing needs.
articles.author: FileMock

articles.title: sitemap.js 9.0: Sitemap Generation CLI Tool and Node.js Library
articles.description: XML ‘sitemap’ files help search spiders (e.g. Googlebot) crawl your site and you can offload the boring task of generating them to this library/CLI if your current system doesn't produce them for you.
articles.author: Eugene Kalinin

articles.title: Horizon: The Current Sky at Your Approximate Location, Rendered as a CSS Gradient
articles.description: In other words, if you view it at night it will just be a black page! 😅 You can see the result here when the sun is out. In old school fashion, it uses meta http-equiv="Refresh" (a trick Drudge Report still uses to this day) to stay up to date and is rendered server-side. We dig it.
articles.author: Daniel Lazaro

articles.title: The Scope Creep
articles.description: An amusing ‘choose-your-own-adventure’ style interactive narrative game. You assume the role of a project manager tasked with delivering a 'cursed' website brief for one of your clients. You’ve got to navigate the project phases, make choices, and face the inevitable issues.
articles.author: Wondermake
